name: From the Beninar Reservoir to Murtas — circular route through the south of the Alpujarras
name_original: Desde el Embalse de Benínar hasta Murtas — circular por el sud de las Alpujarras
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Ugíjar are situated within the Alpujarra Granadina, offering a diverse topography that ranges from lush valleys to high-altitude areas. The region is characterized by mountain landscapes, views of the Sierra Nevada, and unique geological formations like red clay wadis. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, including both challenging ascents and scenic descents through river valleys. Best road cycling routes around Ugíjar The most popular road cycling route is From the Beninar Reservoir to Murtas — circular route through the south of the Alpujarras, a 43.7 miles (70.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through the southern Alpujarras. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ugíjar – Válor Village loop from Cherín, a difficult 37.5 miles (60.4 km) path. This route navigates through the varied landscapes of the Alpujarra, connecting charming villages. Local road cyclists also love the Road to Trevélez (East Side) – Ugíjar loop from Ugíjar, a 50.6 miles (81.4 km) trail leading through the eastern side of the Alpujarra, often completed in about 4 hours 32 minutes. Road cycling around Ugíjar is defined by the dramatic landscapes of the Alpujarra, views of the Sierra Nevada mountains, and winding roads through river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Ugíjar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Ugíjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Ugíjar?
A: There are 10 dedicated road cycling routes around Ugíjar, offering a variety of experiences through the diverse landscapes of the Alpujarra Granadina. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on road cycling routes in Ugíjar?
A: Road cycling in Ugíjar offers a diverse topography, ranging from lush valleys to high-altitude areas. You can expect varied terrain with both challenging ascents and scenic descents. Most routes are classified as difficult, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984105835/from-the-beninar-reservoir-to-murtas-circular-route-through-the-south-of-the-alpujarras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the Beninar Reservoir to Murtas — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while road cycling near Ugíjar?
A: The region offers stunning views of the majestic Sierra Nevada mountains and unique geological formations like red clay wadis. You'll also pass through the picturesque Alpujarra Granadina. Notable landmarks include mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5420457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Ragua&lt;/a&gt; and charming settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4878845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Láujar de Andarax&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5290279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yegen&lt;/a&gt;, which add cultural interest to your ride.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting near Ugíjar?
A: Yes, several routes offer circular options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984105835/from-the-beninar-reservoir-to-murtas-circular-route-through-the-south-of-the-alpujarras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the Beninar Reservoir to Murtas&lt;/a&gt; is a circular route through the southern Alpujarras. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39545321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road to Trevélez (East Side) – Ugíjar loop from Ugíjar&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the eastern side of the Alpujarra.

Q: What are some of the longer and more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14617414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landscape of Las Alpujarras – Ugíjar loop from Ugíjar&lt;/a&gt; covers over 84 km with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ragua Pass – View of La Calahorra Castle loop from Ugíjar&lt;/a&gt; is another demanding option, featuring over 2,400 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any specific villages or towns worth exploring along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Ugíjar itself is an important urban center with Moorish charm, including the Ermita Santa Lucia and the Church of Ugíjar. Nearby, you can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4878845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Láujar de Andarax&lt;/a&gt;, known for its 'Cathedral of the Alpujarras' church, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5290279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yegen&lt;/a&gt;, once home to writer Gerald Brenan. These villages offer cultural stops and opportunities for refreshment.

Q: What amenities are available for cyclists in Ugíjar and nearby towns?
A: Ugíjar provides essential amenities such as supermarkets, restaurants, and bars where you can refuel. In towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4878845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Láujar de Andarax&lt;/a&gt;, you can find squares with bar terraces, perfect for a break after a ride. The region's focus on outdoor sports means you'll find a welcoming atmosphere for cyclists.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Ugíjar?
A: The diverse topography of the Alpujarra Granadina, with its high-altitude areas, suggests that spring and autumn offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling. These seasons typically provide milder temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder conditions or snow at higher elevations in winter.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes around Ugíjar?
A: While many routes in the Ugíjar area are classified as difficult due to the mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes, there is one moderate route available. The region is generally known for its challenging climbs, making it more suitable for intermediate to advanced road cyclists.

Q: What makes the road cycling experience in Ugíjar unique?
A: The unique appeal of road cycling in Ugíjar lies in its dramatic landscapes, offering immense panoramas of the Sierra Nevada mountains and the distinct red clay wadis. The routes wind through charming white villages and river valleys, providing a blend of natural beauty, cultural exploration, and challenging climbs characteristic of the Alpujarra region.

Q: Can I find parking options for my car and bike in Ugíjar?
A: Ugíjar is an important urban center in the Alpujarra Granadina, and as such, you can typically find parking within the town. While specific dedicated cyclist parking facilities are not detailed, general public parking areas should be available for visitors looking to start their rides from Ugíjar.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Yes, the routes often provide breathtaking views of the Sierra Nevada mountains and the varied landscapes of the Alpujarra. Passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5420457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Ragua&lt;/a&gt; are renowned for their panoramas. The winding roads through river valleys and the unique geological formations also offer numerous scenic spots for cyclists to enjoy.
tips: Population geographically located in the center of the Alpujarras, and where it is one of the centers that make up this well-known area of the province of Granada. From this point you can go to a large number of surrounding towns, headed by Sierra Nevada.
Small road from the center of Lucainena, passing through Darrícal and afterwards, ending at the Presa de Benínar. The narrow road but surrounded by beautiful places and that follows the Río Ugíjar.

Reservoir fed by the waters of the Grande de Adra river that descends from Sierra Nevada. The name of the reservoir is due to the fact that it was built on the town of Beninar, which has been left under its waters. The area is surrounded by mountains and rich vegetation, offers a spectacular view of nature.

Murtas is one of the last municipalities in the province of Granada on its southern side, where it is located above the valley of the Río Turón, with great views of the valley and the slopes of the Sierra de Gádor.
